questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Answer the following by appropriately matching the lists based on the information given in the paragraph. Let $f(x)$ is defined as $f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\sin (x+\alpha) & \sin (x+\beta) & \sin (x+\gamma) \\ \cos (x+\alpha) & \cos (x+\beta) & \cos (x+\gamma) \\ \cos (\beta-\gamma) & \cos (\gamma-\alpha) & \cos (\alpha-\beta)\end{array}\right|$ and $f(9)=\lambda \neq 0$ and $P=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\cos (\pi / 9) & \sin (\pi / 9) \\ -\sin (\pi / 9) & \cos (\pi / 9)\end{array}\right]$, where $\alpha, \beta$ and $\gamma$ be non-zero numbers such that $\left(\alpha P^6+\beta P^3+\gamma I\right)$ is the zero matrix and where $I$ is an identity matrix of order 2 .
Question: Which of the following options has the incorrect combination considering List-I and List-II?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad f^{\prime}(x)=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\text { constant } \therefore f(9)=\lambda \\ & \frac{\sum_{k=1}^9 f(k)}{f(9)}=\frac{9 \lambda}{\lambda}=9 \\ & P^2=P \cdot P=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\cos (2 \pi / 9) & \sin (2 \pi / 9) \\ -\sin (2 \pi / 9) & \cos (2 \pi / 9)\end{array}\right] \\ & \therefore \quad P^n=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\cos (n \pi / 9) & \sin (n \pi / 9) \\ -\sin (n \pi / 9) & \cos (n \pi / 9)\end{array}\right] \\ & \alpha P^6+\beta P^3+\gamma I=\alpha\left[\begin{array}{cc}-1 / 2 & \sqrt{3} / 2 \\ -\sqrt{3} / 2 & -1 / 2\end{array}\right]+\beta\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 / 2 & \sqrt{3} / 2 \\ -\sqrt{3} / 2 & 1 / 2\end{array}\right]+\gamma\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=0 \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{-\alpha}{2}+\frac{\beta}{2}+\gamma=0 \quad \text { and } \quad \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}(\alpha+\beta)=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \beta=-\alpha \\ & \therefore \gamma=a \\ & \begin{array}{llll}\therefore & \text { (II) } \mathrm{P} ; & \text { (III) } \mathrm{P} \text { and } & \text { (IV) } \mathrm{Q}\end{array} \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $A$ is a nilpotent matrix of index 2 , then for any positive integer $n, A(I+A)^n$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & A^2=0, A^3=A^4=\ldots=A^n=0 \\ & \text { then } A(I \pm A)^n=A(I+n A) \\ & =A \pm n A^2=A .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\alpha, \beta$ and $\gamma$ are the roots of the equation $x^2(p x+q)=r(x+1)$. Then the value of determinant $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1+\alpha & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1+\beta & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & 1+\gamma\end{array}\right|$ is
questions.solution:
The given equation is $\mathrm{px}^3+\mathrm{qx}^2-\mathrm{rx}-\mathrm{r}=0$. Its roots are given $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ so we have $\alpha+\beta+\gamma=\frac{-q}{p} ; \alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha=-\frac{r}{p} ; \alpha \beta \gamma=\frac{r}{p}$.
$D=\alpha \beta \gamma\left(1+\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta}+\frac{1}{\gamma}\right)=\alpha \beta \gamma\left(\frac{\alpha \beta \gamma+\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha}{\alpha \beta \gamma}\right)=0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Match the following Column-I with Column-II
questions.solution:
(A) $|A-x I|=0$
There exists a non zero matrix $X$ such that $A X=x X$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow|(\operatorname{Adj} A) A| X=x(\operatorname{adj} A) X \\
& \Rightarrow|A| I X=x(\operatorname{adj} A) X \\
& \Rightarrow|A| X=x(\operatorname{adj} A) X \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{|A| X}{x}=(\operatorname{adj} A) X \\
& \Rightarrow(\operatorname{adj} A) X=\frac{|A| X}{2}
\end{aligned}$
(B) $\left|\bar{A}^{-}-\bar{\lambda}\right|=|\overline{(A-\lambda 1)}|=|\overline{A-\lambda .1}|$
$\therefore\left|\bar{A}^{\prime}-\bar{A}\right|=0$ if and only if $|\overline{A-\lambda I}|=0$
$\Rightarrow|\bar{A}-\bar{\lambda}|=0$ if and only if $|\overline{A-i}|=0$
or $\bar{\lambda}$ is the root of $\left|\bar{A}^{\prime}-\mathrm{xI}\right|=0$ if and only if $\lambda$ is the root of $|A-y I|=0$
$\therefore \mathrm{e}^{-1 \alpha}$ is the required solution
(C)
$|B-\lambda I|=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}\left|A_{11}\right|-\lambda\left|A_{12}\right| \ldots \ldots . . A_{1 n} \mid \\ 0 & \left|A_{22}\right|-\lambda \ldots . . A_{2 n} \mid \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 & \mid \\ 1 & 1 & \left|A_{n n}\right|-\lambda\end{array}\right]=0$
$\Rightarrow\left(\left|\mathrm{A}_{11}\right|-\lambda\right)\left(\left|\mathrm{A}_{22}\right|-\lambda\right) \ldots \ldots\left(\left|\mathrm{A}_{\text {nn }}\right|-\lambda\right)-0$
Clearly the elements of principal diagonal become the roots
(D) Let, $A X=\lambda X[\mathrm{X}$ is a non zero matrix $]$
$\Rightarrow \bar{X}^{\prime} \mathrm{AX}=\bar{X}^{\prime} \lambda \mathrm{X}=\lambda \bar{X}^{\prime}=\mathrm{A} \bar{X}^{\prime} \cdot \mathrm{X}$
$\bar{X}^{\prime} A X$ and $\bar{X}^{\prime} I X$ are both real
Also. $\bar{X}^{\prime} X \neq 0, X \neq 0$
$\Rightarrow \lambda=\overline{\mathrm{X}} \wedge \mathrm{X} / \overline{\mathrm{X}} \mathrm{IX}$ is real so $i$ can have real values,
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Paragraph: Answer the following by appropriately matching the lists based on the information given in the paragraph. Let $f(x)$ is defined as $f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\sin (x+\alpha) & \sin (x+\beta) & \sin (x+\gamma) \\ \cos (x+\alpha) & \cos (x+\beta) & \cos (x+\gamma) \\ \cos (\beta-\gamma) & \cos (\gamma-\alpha) & \cos (\alpha-\beta)\end{array}\right|$ and $f(9)=\lambda \neq 0$ and $P=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\cos (\pi / 9) & \sin (\pi / 9) \\ -\sin (\pi / 9) & \cos (\pi / 9)\end{array}\right]$, where $\alpha, \beta$ and $\gamma$ be non-zero numbers such that $\left(\alpha P^6+\beta P^3+\gamma I\right)$ is the zero matrix and where $I$ is an identity matrix of order 2 .
Question: Which of the following options has the correct combination considering List-I and List-II?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad f^{\prime}(x)=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad f(x)=\text { constant } \therefore f(9)=\lambda \\ & \frac{\sum_{k=1}^9 f(k)}{f(9)}=\frac{9 \lambda}{\lambda}=9 \\ & P^2=P \cdot P=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\cos (2 \pi / 9) & \sin (2 \pi / 9) \\ -\sin (2 \pi / 9) & \cos (2 \pi / 9)\end{array}\right] \\ & \therefore \quad P^n=\left[\begin{array}{cc}\cos (n \pi / 9) & \sin (n \pi / 9) \\ -\sin (n \pi / 9) & \cos (n \pi / 9)\end{array}\right] \\ & \alpha P^6+\beta P^3+\gamma I=\alpha\left[\begin{array}{cc}-1 / 2 & \sqrt{3} / 2 \\ -\sqrt{3} / 2 & -1 / 2\end{array}\right]+\beta\left[\begin{array}{cc}1 / 2 & \sqrt{3} / 2 \\ -\sqrt{3} / 2 & 1 / 2\end{array}\right]+\gamma\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=0 \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{-\alpha}{2}+\frac{\beta}{2}+\gamma=0 \quad \text { and } \quad \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}(\alpha+\beta)=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \beta=-\alpha \\ & \therefore \gamma=a \\ & \begin{array}{llll}\therefore & \text { (II) } \mathrm{P} ; & \text { (III) } \mathrm{P} \text { and } & \text { (IV) } \mathrm{Q}\end{array} \\ & \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $A$ and $B$ are square matrices of order 3 where $|A|=-2$ and $|B|=1$ then $\left|\left(A^{-1}\right) \operatorname{adj}\left(B^{-1}\right) \operatorname{adj}\left(2 A^{-1}\right)\right|$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have $\left|\left(A^{-1}\right) \operatorname{adj}\left(B^{-1}\right) \operatorname{adj}\left(2 A^{-1}\right)\right|=\frac{1}{|A|} \cdot \frac{1}{|B|^2} \cdot \frac{64}{|A|^2}=\frac{64}{-2 \times 1 \times 4}=-8$
(By using $\left|\mathrm{A}^{-1}\right|=\frac{1}{|\mathrm{~A}|}$ and $|\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{B}|=|\mathrm{B}|^{\mathrm{n}-1}$ where n is order of square matrix)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider an identity matrix $(n \times n) I_n ; \lambda \in R^{+}$then $\left|\operatorname{Adj}\left(\lambda I_n\right)\right|$ is
questions.solution:
$\left(\lambda I_n\right) \operatorname{Adj}\left(\lambda I_n\right)=\left|\lambda I_n\right| I$
$\Rightarrow \operatorname{Adj}\left(\lambda I_n\right)=\lambda^{n-1} I_n$
$\Rightarrow\left|\operatorname{Adj}\left(\lambda I_n\right)\right|=\lambda^{n(n-1)}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
